optimizer_switches.name: index_condition_pushdown
optimizer_switches.explanation: Parts of the WHERE clause are evaluated inside the storage engine using index columns before fetching the full row — reduces rows handed to the server layer.

optimizer_switches.name: materialization
optimizer_switches.explanation: A subquery or derived table is executed once and its result stored in a temporary table for reuse. Watch tmp_table_size / max_heap_table_size — it may spill to disk.

warnings.text: Full table scan: <temporary> (10 rows), org_chart (1000 rows)
1 temp table(s) (Materialize) — up to 1000 rows; may spill to disk
1 sort operation(s) — 10 rows; may use disk-based filesort

suggestions.action: Add indexes on filter/join columns to avoid full table scans
Increase tmp_table_size / max_heap_table_size to keep temp tables in memory
Increase sort_buffer_size or add an ordered index to avoid filesort

executive_summary: Query scans 2 tables, materializes 1 temp table and sorts the result; examines ~601 rows to return 10 in 1.6 ms. Main finding: full scan of org_chart (1,000 rows).

query.raw: with recursive `org_chart` as (/* select#2 */ select `demodb`.`employees`.`id` AS `id`,`demodb`.`employees`.`name` AS `name`,`demodb`.`employees`.`manager_id` AS `manager_id`,1 AS `level` from `demodb`.`employees` where (`demodb`.`employees`.`manager_id` is null) union all /* select#3 */ select `e`.`id` AS `id`,`e`.`name` AS `name`,`e`.`manager_id` AS `manager_id`,(`oc`.`level` + 1) AS `oc.level + 1` from `demodb`.`employees` `e` join `org_chart` `oc` where ((`e`.`manager_id` = `oc`.`id`) and (`oc`.`level` < 10))) /* select#1 */ select `org_chart`.`level` AS `level`,count(0) AS `headcount` from `org_chart` group by `org_chart`.`level` order by `org_chart`.`level`
query.beautified:
with recursive org_chart as (/*
select#2 */
select demodb.employees.id AS id,demodb.employees.name AS name,demodb.employees.manager_id AS manager_id,1 AS level
from demodb.employees
where (demodb.employees.manager_id is null)
union all /*
select#3 */
select e.id AS id,e.name AS name,e.manager_id AS manager_id,(oc.level + 1) AS oc.level + 1
from demodb.employees e
  join org_chart oc
where ((e.manager_id = oc.id) and (oc.level < 10))) /*
select#1 */
select org_chart.level AS level,count(0) AS headcount
from org_chart
group by org_chart.level
order by org_chart.level
